title: CF Industries (CF) to Report Q2 Earnings: What's in Store?
text: CF Industries' (CF) Q2 performance is likely to have benefited from increasing global demand for nitrogen fertilizers. Weaker nitrogen prices are likely to have hurt the company's sales and margins in the quarter.

title: CF Fertilisers UK Announces Proposal to Permanently Close Ammonia Plant at Billingham Complex
text: BILLINGHAM, U.K.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--CF Fertilisers UK Limited, a subsidiary of CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: CF), today announced a proposal to permanently close the ammonia plant at its Billingham Complex in order to secure the long-term sustainability of its business in the United Kingdom (U.K.) and more efficiently serve its customers in the country. The Company intends to continue to produce ammonium nitrate (AN) fertiliser and nitric acid at the Billingham site using imported ammonia,.

title: CF Industries Adds Clean Ammonia Growth Prospects
text: CF Industries is a $15 billion market cap ammonia and nitrogen company offering a 2.1% dividend. With now-lower feedstock and energy costs, its results are expected to improve. High natural gas costs and lower demand dragged down its first quarter results; however, restocking and new demand for ammonia in energy applications suggests future upside.

title: CF Industries Announces Senior Executive Appointments
text: DEERFIELD, Ill.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: CF) today announced that Douglas C. Barnard, Senior Vice President, General Counsel and Secretary, has informed the Company that he will retire from CF Industries January 12, 2024. Effective immediately, Mr. Barnard will serve as Executive Vice President, Corporate Development and Legal Advisor, reporting to Tony Will, President and Chief Executive Officer, CF Industries Holdings, Inc., with primary responsibility for obtaini.

title: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. Declares Quarterly Dividend and Confirms Dates for First Half and Second Quarter 2023 Results and Conference Call
text: DEERFIELD, Ill.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: CF) today reported that its board of directors has declared a $0.40 per share dividend on its common stock. The dividend will be payable on August 31, 2023, to stockholders of record as of August 15, 2023. Additionally, the Company confirmed that it will report its first half and second quarter 2023 results after the market close on Wednesday, August 2, 2023. The company plans to host a conference call to discuss these result.

title: CF Industries: Beneficial Market Condition And Better Than Peers' Performance
text: CF Industries Holdings saw a significant increase in stock price and net sales in 2022 due to high ammonia and urea prices caused by the Ukraine war disrupting fertilizer supply. However, nitrogen product prices decreased in the second half of 2022 and first half of 2023, leading to a 30% YoY decrease in CF's net sales in 1Q 2023. I expect the company's 2Q 2023 financial results to be weaker than in 1Q 2023. However, it is not the result of bad performance or bad management.

title: CF Industries: Downside Seems Limited And Rewards Plenty
text: CF Industries Holdings Inc, a global producer and supplier of nitrogen fertilizer, has seen a decline in sales due to decreased product prices, despite steady demand. The company has a strong financial position, with a solid cash position covering almost all long-term debts, and a net debt/EBITDA ratio of just 0.07. Despite a volatile short-term outlook, the company's strong cash flows, large buyback plan and strong margins compared to competitors make it a strong investment opportunity.

title: Safety First: 3 Dividend Stocks for Investors Seeking Stability
text: With the most recent read on inflation seemingly suggesting a trajectory of disinflation, the very concept of dividend stocks for stability seems overly cautious. Fundamentally, I would gently suggest a rethink of this framework.

title: The 7 Most Undervalued S&P 500 Stocks to Buy Now
text: Finding deals in undervalued S&P 500 stocks is similar to finding deals in anything. It always raises the same question: Why is this priced lower than it should be?

title: 3 Boring Stocks That You Should Buy and Hold Forever
text: To be quite blunt, the very idea of proposing boring stocks to buy seems absolutely stupid in most circumstances. Not to break the fourth wall or anything but I have a feeling you didn't come to read InvestorPlace articles because you wanted to hear a pitch about slow-and-steady risers.
